title: Will Positive Gross Profit Turn Rivian Stock Around?
text: According to The Motley Fool analysis, reaching positive gross profit alone will not be sufficient to revive Rivian's struggling stock. While Rivian expects to achieve modest gross profit in Q4 2024, this milestone falls far short of what investors need to see. Gross profit accounts for the cost of goods sold, a major expense for automakers, but doesn't address operating expenses, depreciation, amortization, or net income. Rivian's cash position has plummeted from over $18 billion to under $8 billion since going public. The company must progress through gross profit to operating profit, then to positive earnings per share. Investors' patience is being tested, and the company is stressing its balance sheet. Rivian remains in prove it mode, requiring fundamental improvements across profitability metrics before the stock becomes attractive. Simply achieving gross profitability represents progress but doesn't guarantee the company can generate sustainable free cash flow or reach true profitability.
